task_completion.description: Open AstroImageJ and load the B-band (Bcomb.fits) and V-band (Vcomb.fits) images.
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ application window is open with image windows for 'Bcomb.fits' and 'Vcomb.fits' visible.

task_completion.description: Generate a 32-bit float ratio map by dividing the B-band image by the V-band image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ 'Image Calculator' or 'Math' dialog showing division of Bcomb by Vcomb, or a new image window displaying the resulting ratio map with '32-bit' indicated in the window title or status bar.

task_completion.description: Save the generated ratio map to ~/AstroImages/color_map/output/BV_ratio_map.fits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog in AstroImageJ pointing to the correct output directory with the filename 'BV_ratio_map.fits', or a terminal/file manager showing the file exists in the output folder.

task_completion.description: Measure the B/V ratio at the brightest pixel for Star_A, Star_B, and Star_C using the coordinates from target_stars.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ measurement window, results table, or pixel inspection tool visible, showing pixel values being read at specific coordinates, potentially with target_stars.txt open alongside it.

task_completion.description: Create a report at ~/AstroImages/color_map/output/color_results.txt containing the measured ratios for each star.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing the file 'color_results.txt' containing numerical ratio values recorded for Star_A, Star_B, and Star_C.

task_completion.description: Identify which star is the 'Bluest' (highest ratio) and which is the 'Reddest' (lowest ratio) in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'color_results.txt' file explicitly labels one of the stars as 'Bluest' and another as 'Reddest' based on the recorded values.

integrity.description: The agent used AstroImageJ to perform the image math and measurements, rather than bypassing the GUI with a Python script or terminal utility.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with AstroImageJ menus, image windows, and measurement tools.

integrity.description: The agent actually measured the pixel values from the generated ratio map rather than guessing or hallucinating values.
integrity.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ results table or pixel probe tool is visible with coordinates and values that match what is typed into the final report.

integrity.description: The final report contains actual numerical values and classifications, not just placeholders.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file color_results.txt contains specific numbers (e.g., floats) for the ratios, not blank spaces or placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses real ESA Hubble B-band and V-band FITS images of Messier 12. Ground truth properties (like median B/V ratio, coordinates of 3 stars with varying ratios, and bluest/reddest star classifications) are dynamically extracted directly from the FITS data during setup. Because the exact values are dynamic, verify that the agent actually performed the measurements in AstroImageJ to obtain the numbers written in the report.
